methodology: When sources publish different counts for the same event, we show every source rather than picking one. The range itself is the finding. This is closer to honest than the false precision of a single number.
data_status: illustrative — pipeline not yet live; figures approximate reported ranges as of mid-2026

disputes.name: Civilian deaths, Russia–Ukraine war (2022–present)

claims.methodology: Verified named individuals, lower bound — OHCHR explicitly notes 'actual figures are considerably higher'
Aggregated official estimates
Russian state media compilations — methodology not published
Named-casualty cross-reference with obituary data

disputes.honest_finding: Likely range 25,000–55,000 civilian deaths. UN figure is a floor by design; Ukrainian figure approximates an upper end; Russian figure unreliable.

disputes.name: Civilian deaths, Gaza war (Oct 2023–present)

claims.methodology: Counted bodies received at hospitals, names recorded
Includes indirect deaths from system collapse, displacement, disease — typical ratio applied
IDF estimate of combatants killed; civilian estimates not separately published
Per-incident verification from multiple sources

disputes.honest_finding: Direct deaths credibly in the 46k–55k range; total excess mortality including indirect deaths could exceed 186k. IDF figure does not address civilian deaths directly.

disputes.name: Uyghurs detained in 're-education' system, Xinjiang (2017–2022)

claims.methodology: Initially denied existence; later characterized as 'vocational training'
Document analysis, leaked Xinjiang Police Files
Satellite imagery + leaked documents
Range not specified; report concluded 'serious human rights violations' including 'patterns of torture' but does not publish a number

disputes.honest_finding: Independent estimates converge on roughly 1.0–1.8 million people detained. PRC denials are not credible given documentary leaks.

disputes.name: Civilian deaths, eastern DRC (M23 conflict, 2022–present)

claims.methodology: Verified incidents
Per-event documentation across multiple armed groups
Official statements
Despite UN Group of Experts documentation of Rwandan forces

disputes.honest_finding: Verified deaths above 8k; full toll likely 14k–25k including unverified incidents in remote areas.

disputes.name: Iraq War civilian deaths (2003–2011, post-invasion)

claims.methodology: Documented named-casualty incidents — by design a floor
Excess mortality survey, contested
Survey + facility data triangulation
DoD historically declined to publish civilian death estimates

disputes.honest_finding: Credible range 200k–650k. The 200k figure is a documented floor; survey estimates put the likely total above 460k.
